claim_text: Scrapling 0.4.x added a Scrapy-style spider framework with start_urls, parse(), Request/Response, lifecycle hooks, and async iteration.
Top-level public API exports in 0.4.8 are: Selector, Fetcher, AsyncFetcher, StealthyFetcher, DynamicFetcher.
notes: Single-source but primary (the source code itself). A1 grade.

claim_text: PlayWrightFetcher does not exist in 0.4.x — it was renamed to DynamicFetcher.
The scrapling mcp server exposes 10 tools: open_session, close_session, list_sessions, get, bulk_get, fetch, bulk_fetch, stealthy_fetch, bulk_stealthy_fetch, screenshot.
notes: Single source but the source code itself (FastMCP add_tool calls). Highest possible primary.

claim_text: scrapling mcp defaults to stdio transport; --http enables streamable-http transport.
scrapling mcp --http defaults to host 0.0.0.0:8000, which is unsafe for shared-network use.
notes: Code fact is single-sourced from CLI source. Security implication is our inference. Moved to Needs Verification.

claim_text: Scrapling's anti-detection stack combines curl_cffi (TLS/JA3), patchright (Playwright stealth fork), and browserforge (fingerprint generation).
Scrapling bypasses Cloudflare Turnstile/Interstitial in the typical case.
notes: Maintainer claim; no independent reproduction in our source set but no counter-claim either.

claim_text: Scrapling's bypass against Tencent WAF is probabilistic — not deterministic.
notes: Issue #265 with concrete reproduction script. Primary evidence.

claim_text: StealthyFetcher's patchright uses its own .local-browsers cache directory, causing a second browser download even when Playwright browsers are installed.
The README's 35-620x speed claim is against MechanicalSoup and BS4+html5lib — known-slow choices.
Against Parsel/Scrapy and raw lxml, Scrapling is at parity (~2ms on a 5000-element synthetic doc).
selectolax (Lexbor C engine) is materially faster than Scrapling for pure parser throughput.
robots.txt obedience is built into Scrapling via the protego library (Scrapy's robots parser).
Scrapling has a built-in ProxyRotator with cyclic or custom strategies plus per-request proxy override.
Scrapling provides persistent sessions (FetcherSession, DynamicSession, StealthySession) preserving cookies and storage across requests.
Scrapling is actively maintained as of May 2026 (commits May 11/16/18 2026).
Scrapling is a single-maintainer project (Karim Shoair / D4Vinci); bus factor = 1.
notes: Author field from PyPI; structural risk inference is our framing.

claim_text: Crawlee/Scrapy-based comparisons in 2026 typically still classify Scrapling as a parser library, missing the 0.4 framework additions.
Scrapling supports HTTP/3 via curl_cffi in Fetcher.
Scrapling CLI provides install, mcp (--http/--host/--port), shell, and extract subcommands (get/post/put/delete/fetch/stealth).
For maximum parser throughput on static HTML, selectolax is preferable to Scrapling.
For distributed crawl coordination beyond a single host, Scrapy + scrapy-redis remains the leader.
Scrapling's adaptive matcher overhead stays in the same performance range as the rest of the parser (~2.39ms in the README benchmark).
